reasoning: The custom_css value at line 112 of wf-flyout.php is sourced exclusively from config.php, a static hardcoded PHP array literal shipped with the plugin. No attacker-controlled input (HTTP request, database, user-supplied data) flows into $this->config['custom_css'] at any point in the codebase. The finding's own reachability statement acknowledges this: exploitation requires filesystem write access, which collapses this to a post-exploitation scenario rather than a web vulnerability. Additionally, the sanitization chain is double-layered: sanitize_text_field() (which strips HTML tags) is applied to custom_css before concatenation, and then wp_kses_wf() applies wp_kses() with an allowlist to the entire $out string, which would strip any unexpected script tags even if sanitize_text_field() missed them. The sink is only reached in admin_head on admin-only plugin screens, further reducing impact. There is no real vulnerability here.
